sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.18 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: (B.\,H.\,Neumann). A group$U$is called universal\/ for a class${ K}$of groups if$U$contains an isomorphic image of every member of${ K}$. Does there exist a countable group that is universal for the class of countable orderable groups? { A.\,I.\,Kokorin} No (M.\,I.\,Kargapolov, Algebra and Logic, { 9} (1970), 257--261; \ D.\,B.\,Smith, Pacific J. Math., { 35} (1970), 499--502).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
